denial_reason:
Governed workflow rejected by reviewer agent.
Verdict: flag (confidence 88%)
Findings: SECURITY (low severity in CI): sign-windows.ps1 sets $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop', which causes Write-Error calls inside the foreach loop to throw terminating errors and exit the script before the temp PFX cleanup block runs. Affected paths: artifact-not-found, signtool sign failure, signtool verify failure. The temp PFX (containing the private key and cert) is left on disk until the runner is destroyed. On GitHub Actions ephemeral runners this is acceptable, but violates defense-in-depth. Fix: wrap the signing loop and cleanup in try/finally.; DEAD CODE: $msiPattern is defined in the release.yml inline script but never used — artifact collection uses Get-ChildItem -Filter '*.msi' instead. The variable is harmless but misleading.; PLAN.md items 1 (EV cert procurement) and 8 (macOS notarization) remain open with notes — both are human-action or deferred items, correctly documented and not expected to be closed by this agent.; No Rust code changed; no test coverage gap. Script correctness verified: signtool sign/verify invocation, SHA-256 digest, RFC 3161 timestamp, graceful secret-absent skip, and exit-code propagation all look correct.

Findings: PLAN.md status marker changed from 'in_progress' to 'pending' — CLAUDE.md explicitly forbids agents from changing <!-- status: ... --> markers (only ta draft apply may transition them). The marker should remain 'in_progress'; TA apply will set it to 'done'. Impact is low since apply auto-corrects, but the rule was violated.; is_doc_example() uses lower_val.contains(placeholder) for short generic words ('test', 'fake', 'dummy', 'sample', 'example'). For known-service tokens (Slack xoxb-*, GitHub ghp_*, etc.) the doc-example check runs BEFORE the known_service guard and always wins. A real Slack token whose 36-char payload happens to contain the substring 'test' would be misclassified as DocExample and suppressed. Low probability but real bypass path for callers who control token values.; safe_rel_path() strips fs://workspace/ but falls back to the raw URI string when the prefix is absent (unwrap_or(resource_uri)). Non-workspace URIs (e.g. a future external-resource URI scheme) are silently accepted and treated as relative paths. This is not exploitable now (all staging artifact URIs use fs://workspace/), but could become one if a new URI scheme is added without updating the guard. A defensive explicit rejection of unknown URI schemes would be safer.; Post-commit scan runs git diff HEAD^..HEAD without error handling beyond a single if-let: if the git invocation fails silently (non-UTF8 diff, git binary missing), credentials in the commit diff are never scanned. The pre-apply scan is the primary gate so this is defense-in-depth, but the failure mode is silent.
